Strip_Summary: Bold white text on dark background: 'You arrive second.'
POV from patrol car: gloved hands on wheel, crime scene lights ahead.
Text sequence: 'Thirty-seven scenes. Not one investigation. Not one question.'
Bold text on black: 'By the time you leave, it never happened.'
Rookie cop arriving at night scene, four cruisers, spinning lights, wet streets.
Cop standing by tape, watching. Lights going, nobody rushing.
Senior cop kicks gun closer to body. Doesn't bend down. Nobody reacts.
Senior cop kicks gun closer to body. Doesn't bend down. Nobody reacts. (cont.)
Like clearing a rock off sidewalk. Gun was always there.
EMT writing on clipboard. Nobody looks up. System compliance.
Rookie says nothing. First clean witnessed.
Text: 'Silence. You learn it before you learn anything else.'
Senior detective in wrinkled jacket, parking lot, cigarette smoke, teaching.
Senior detective in wrinkled jacket, parking lot, cigarette smoke, teaching. (cont.)
Not how to read a crime scene. How to read a problem.
Where story falls apart. What camera caught. What report needs to say.
Senior giving directions like gas station route. Turn left, turn right.
Rookie nods, doesn't write anything. Learning the difference.
Watching first clean by someone else. Hands move. Eyes follow.
Senior tilts barrel three degrees. Checks dash cam sightline. Steps like framing photo.
Senior tilts barrel three degrees. Checks dash cam sightline. Steps like framing photo. (cont.)
Senior picks up shell casing between fingers. Pockets it. Writes two words.
Senior picks up shell casing between fingers. Pockets it. Writes two words. (cont.)
Investigation closes in week. Nobody reviews photos. Family gets letter.
Investigation closes in week. Nobody reviews photos. Family gets letter. (cont.)
Rookie hasn't touched anything. But knows how it works now.
Cop sitting in car at night. Radio off. Streetlight buzzing.
Thinking about the hands. Where they were. Where they are now.
Point of no return. Doesn't touch anything. Doesn't report either.
First clean. Simple scene. One body. Hands up, palms open.
First clean. Simple scene. One body. Hands up, palms open. (cont.)
Kneeling on cold asphalt. Latex squeaking. Pulling gloves tight.
Repositioning hands: one on waistband, one reaching. Four seconds.
Repositioning hands: one on waistband, one reaching. Four seconds. (cont.)
Text: 'Four seconds and the scene tells a different story.'
Waiting for it to feel wrong. It doesn't.
Standing up. Brushing gravel. Pull gloves tighter. Moving on.
Standing up. Brushing gravel. Pull gloves tighter. Moving on. (cont.)
Hallway by vending machine. Officer shaken. Shirt untucked. 'Tell me again.'
Hallway by vending machine. Officer shaken. Shirt untucked. 'Tell me again.' (cont.)
Not from when it started. From when you arrived. Officer looks. 'Tell me again.'
Third time story holds. Always takes three. Don't know why.
Filing report. Same form. Scene cleared by midnight.
Driving home. Parking in driveway. Headlights off. Engine ticking.
Driving home. Parking in driveway. Headlights off. Engine ticking. (cont.)
Neighbor porch light. Waiting for someone to call. Detective. Internal affairs.
Neighbor porch light. Waiting for someone to call. Detective. Internal affairs. (cont.)
Checking phone. Nothing. No one calls. No one questions it.
Checking phone. Nothing. No one calls. No one questions it. (cont.)
Text: 'By the time you leave, it never happened.'
Stepping back. Looking at scene. Checking if matches story built. It does.
Stepping back. Looking at scene. Checking if matches story built. It does. (cont.)
Text: 'You did that.'
POV: hands pulling on gloves slowly, one finger at a time. Second skin.
POV: hands pulling on gloves slowly, one finger at a time. Second skin. (cont.)
Radio turned down. Dispatch doesn't need to hear this.
Radio turned down. Dispatch doesn't need to hear this. (cont.)
Text: 'You can't.'
It gets easier. Second clean half the time. Third not counting.
It gets easier. Second clean half the time. Third not counting. (cont.)
Gun says self-defense. Casings scattered in line. Can fix one, not both.
Gun says self-defense. Casings scattered in line. Can fix one, not both. (cont.)
Choose the gun. Always choose the gun.
Close on hands moving gun two inches. Casings left untouched.
Text: 'It always matters later.'
Two inches. That's all it takes.
Text: 'Two inches turns an execution into self-defense.'
Thirty-seven scenes. Not one question. Not one version that wasn't yours.
Autopsy comes back. Bruising doesn't match hand position in photos.
Paper disagrees with scene. Paper always wins.
Calling officer at home. Wife picks up. TV in background. 'Were hands up or down?'
Calling officer at home. Wife picks up. TV in background. 'Were hands up or down?' (cont.)
Down. No. Up. I think up. Long pause.
Wife says something. He covers phone. You change one line.
Hands reaching toward waistband. One line changes everything.
Next month. Body camera on. Pulling battery. Red light blinks twice.
Sync light was on. Cloud upload. Don't know how much got through.
Don't know if saved somewhere unreachable. Casings might come back.
Clean scene anyway. Report will reach them first.
Tell me again. Director giving notes. Skip the hands.
Officer repeats word for word. Nods. Believes it or learned to.
Officer repeats word for word. Nods. Believes it or learned to. (cont.)
Drop gun in trunk where it always stays. Battery died per log.
Camera log filled without looking. Scratch on desk. Following with finger.
Camera log filled without looking. Scratch on desk. Following with finger. (cont.)
Stepping back. Already matches. Knew it would. Shoulders release.
Hands know work before brain. Stop remembering names.
Stop asking what happened. Ask what it needs to look like.
Text: 'And then one day - you don't remember when that changed.'
Text: 'And then one day - you don't remember when that changed.' (cont.)
New call. Arrive. Don't wait. Don't ask. Start working.
Move gun. Pocket casing. Reposition hands.
Like done it before. Too many times.
Then you stop. Knees hurt. Asphalt again.
Read the scene. Shooting justified. Scene was clean.
Everything where it should be. Already changed three things.
Don't know which is worse.
Try to put gun back. Angle wrong. Already been moved.
Someone got here before. Or you were here before. Scene feels familiar.
Text: 'You don't know which is worse.'
Everything goes flat - lights, radio, officer behind - runs together.
Everything goes flat - lights, radio, officer behind - runs together. (cont.)
Blink. It's back. Looking at gloved hands.
Don't remember walking to body. Don't remember starting.
Back at desk. Old report. Hands reaching toward waistband.
Remember reaching. Don't remember open. Because wrote reaching.
Reaching is what you remember now.
Text: 'By the time you leave, it never happened.'
Writing report. Matches what you made. Don't know what was there.
New cop in fresh uniform. Quiet. Keeps hands in pockets. Watching.
New cop in fresh uniform. Quiet. Keeps hands in pockets. Watching. (cont.)
Eyes on hands whole time. Doesn't say anything. Doesn't ask.
Next month moves casing on own. Doesn't announce. You nod.
Don't remember showing him. Asked what he saw: 'I arrived after.'
Six months later. 'Tell me again' from his mouth. Your phrase.
Watching him work. Same angle. Same sightline check. Same framing.
Don't remember learning it. They don't remember asking.
Stepping back. It looks right. Don't know why.
Old report. Own handwriting. Every detail clean.
Can't tell which details are real.
Phone rings 2 AM. Same voice. Same fear. Same silence.
Reaching for gloves on nightstand. Since first year. Dressed before they finish.
Reaching for gloves on nightstand. Since first year. Dressed before they finish. (cont.)
Text: 'It doesn't matter what happened. Only what it needs to look like.'
Cop driving toward new scene. You arrive second. Always do.
Don't remember what 'it' was. Only remember what it looked like.
